popularStory.headline: SIR HENRY HAND (1440-) AND LADY MARGARET BEAUFORT (1442-1509):
popularStory.description:
SIR HENRY HAND was born about 1440 of Kent, England, to Sir Hand of Benenden (1420-) and Unknown. He married Lady Margaret Beaufort.

Sir Henry Hand died of Kent, England, Unknown date.

 LADY MARGARE …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Beaufort於1443年5月31日出生在英國貝德福爾布勒左時，她的父親John Beaufort 1st Duke of Somerset and Earl of Kendal39歲，母親Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset38歲。她於從1444年1月28日到1444年2月7日與John de la Pole結婚。她去世於1509年6月29日在英國倫敦享年66歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Beaufort
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Beaufort . The surname Beauford is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Beaufort was born on 31 May 1443, in Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, England, her father, John Beaufort 1st Duke of Somerset and Earl of Kendal, was 39 and her mother, Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset, was 38. She married John de la Pole from 28 January 1444 to 7 February 1444. She died on 29 June 1509, in London, England, at the age of 66, and was buried in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Beaufort est née le 31 mai 1443, à Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, son père, John Beaufort 1st Duke of Somerset and Earl of Kendal, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset, avait 38 ans. Elle a épousé John de la Pole du 28 janvier 1444 au 7 février 1444. Elle est décédée le 29 juin 1509, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterrée à Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre.

Mai 1443 in Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Beaufort 1st Duke of Somerset and Earl of Kendal 39 und ihre Mutter Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset 38. Sie hat von 28. Januar 1444 bis 7. Februar 1444 John de la Pole geheiratet. Sie starb am 29. Juni 1509 im Alter von 66 Jahren in London, England und wurde in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Beaufortが1443年5月31日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアブレトソで生まれた時，父John Beaufort 1st Duke of Somerset and Earl of Kendalは39歳，母Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somersetは38歳であった。から1444年1月28日まで1444年2月7日にJohn de la Poleと結婚した。彼女は1509年6月29日にイギリスロンドンで66歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターウエストミンスター アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Beaufort(이)가 1443년 5월 31일 영국 베드퍼드실르 블레츠에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Beaufort 1st Duke of Somerset and Earl of Kendal(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 1444년 1월 28일부터 1444년 2월 7일까지 John de la Pole(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1509년 6월 29일 영국 런던에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Beaufort nasceu em 31 de maio de 1443, em Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Beaufort 1st Duke of Somerset and Earl of Kendal, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset, tinha 38 anos. Ela casou-se com John de la Pole de 28 de janeiro de 1444 a 7 de fevereiro de 1444. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Beaufort родилась 31 мая 1443 г. в местности блецо, бедфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Beaufort 1st Duke of Somerset and Earl of Kendal было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset, 38. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de la Pole с 28 января 1444 по 7 февраля 1444. Она умерла 29 июня 1509 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 66 лет и была похоронена в местности вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия.
